source: Claude gap analysis 2026-06-11 (Track D — identity accretes from lived history)
title: Identity-from-memory: distill SOLUTIONS lessons + L2 self-facts into a compact self-model block
current_state: Pi's 101 solutions and months of L2 facts contain its actual lived identity — recurring bug classes it has learned (write/read divergence), preferences Ash has expressed, what it is good and bad at. None of that reaches the prompt; identity is whatever consciousness.txt asserted months ago.
target_state: A weekly distillation (hook into retro.py's Friday cadence) produces vault/memory/L3/self_model.md (~30 lines): top recurring root-cause classes, confirmed strengths/weaknesses with ticket evidence, active commitments. It is promoted into the WARM prompt segment (~200 token cap) like other L3 ambient context. Pi introduces changes to itself with evidence ('write/read divergence is my recurring bug class — 12 solutions') instead of static prose.
migration_plan: 1. retro.py gains build_self_model(): Groq tier='cheap' over SOLUTIONS.jsonl root_causes + L2 category='self' facts → structured 30-line md.
2. Write as a pinned L3 entry (category='self_model', high importance, refreshed not appended — dedup by category).
3. Token cap enforced at write time; get_l3_context already budget-aware.
4. Tests: distillation output respects cap; refresh replaces not duplicates; degraded path (no Groq) keeps last version.
risk_notes: Free-tier Groq cost only. The dedup-not-append detail is the L3 duplicate class — test it.
